normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def lcsLen (a b : List Int) : Nat := sorry
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the function that returns the length of the longest common subsequence between lists a and b.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def IsSubseq (s t : List Int) : Prop := ∃ (pref suf : List Int), t = pref ++ s ++ suf ∧ True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Provide a predicate capturing that s is a subsequence of t (not necessarily contiguous) in the same relative order.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ s t : List Int, IsSubseq s t ↔ (∃ f : Fin s.length → Fin t.length, (∀ i, f i < t.length) ∧ (∀ i j, i < j → f i < f j) ∧ (∀ i, s.nthLe i (by simp) = t.nthLe (f i) (by simp)))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Characterize subsequence by an order-preserving index embedding from s into t.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ s : List Int, IsSubseq s a → IsSubseq s b → s.length ≤ lcsLen a b
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Any common subsequence s of a and b has length at most the returned value.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∃ s : List Int, IsSubseq s a ∧ IsSubseq s b ∧ s.length = lcsLen a b
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists a common subsequence whose length equals the returned value.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ n : Nat, (∃ s : List Int, IsSubseq s a ∧ IsSubseq s b ∧ s.length = n) → n ≤ lcsLen a b
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned length is the maximum length among all common subsequences.
If either input list is empty, the longest common subsequence length is 0.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ a b : List Int, ∀ x y : Int, a = aInit ++ [x] → b = bInit ++ [y] → x = y → lcsLen a b = lcsLen aInit bInit + 1
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the last elements of both lists are equal, the optimal length increases by 1 over the prefixes.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ a b : List Int, ∀ x y : Int, a = aInit ++ [x] → b = bInit ++ [y] → x ≠ y → lcsLen a b = Nat.max (lcsLen aInit b) (lcsLen a bInit)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the last elements differ, the optimal length is the maximum of dropping one last element from either list.
